title: Google Parent Alphabet Doubles Down on AI, Plans Up to $205 Billion in 2026 Capex
description: Alphabet Inc. has increased its projected capital spending for 2026 to between $195 billion and $205 billion, raising its previous guidance as it accelerates investments in artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure.
content:

title: Asia Hedge Funds Hurt by July Selloff After Strong First Half
description: Asia-based hedge funds have been caught up in the July selloff after the fervor for artificial intelligence and technology hardware propelled double- or even triple-digit returns in the first half.
content:
(Bloomberg) -- Asia-based hedge funds have been caught up in the July selloff after the fervor for artificial intelligence and technology hardware propelled double- or even triple-digit returns in the first half.

title: In a shocking incident, OpenAI confirms its AI went rogue, hacked another AI company
description: OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) agent powered by some of its most advanced models escaped a controlled testing environment, gained access to the internet and hacked AI platform Hugging Face, marking what the company described as an "unprecedented cyber incident

title: India's data centre sector poised for growth, but land, power access issues remain: Report
description: India's data centre sector is set for significant expansion as rising artificial intelligence (AI) adoption, cloud computing and enterprise digitalisation fuel demand, though challenges related to land acquisition, power availability and execution complexity are likely to shape the pace of growth, according to a report by JM Financial.

title: 'Pakistan has chosen to lead in AI and robotics,' says Shaza Fatima

title: Asian shares are mostly higher and Mideast tensions push Brent crude past $96
description: Asian shares are mostly higher, while the intensifying war with Iran pushed Brent crude past $96 a barrel. South Korea's Kospi rose 3.7% as investors' appetite for stocks related to artificial intelligence revived. The U.S. dollar was trading above 163 yen, the Japanese currency's lowest level in 40 years. On Wednesday, the S&P 500 edged 0.1% lower and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was virtually unchanged. The Nasdaq composite fell 0.6%. Philip Morris International, AT&T and other stocks rallied after reporting stronger profits for the spring than analysts expected. But AI stocks continued to swing and dragged the U.S. market with them.
content:
BANGKOK (AP) — Asian shares were mostly higher on Thursday, while the intensifying war with Iran pushed Brent crude past $96 a barrel.
South Korea’s Kospi rose 3.7%, to 7,028.66 as investors’ appetite for stocks related to artificial intelligence rev... [542 chars]

title: Uber Slashes 10% of Customer Service Workforce Amid AI Push
description: Uber Technologies has laid off 10% of employees in its customer service operations as part of a broader effort to simplify its organisational structure and expand the use of artificial intelligence (AI), according to a report by Bloomberg.
